symbols: child among wild animals — the little child who shall lead them in Isaiah's prophecy
lion lying down with ox — the enmity of nature healed in the messianic age
wolf with lamb — the predator-prey relationship reversed by divine peace
leopard with kid — even the most dangerous creatures made gentle
river landscape in background — the peaceable land of promise
description: A pastoral scene with text borders showing a child seated among a gathering of wild and domestic animals living in harmony. A lion, a leopard, a bear, a wolf, oxen, lambs, and goats all gather peacefully around a central child figure. The animals show no aggression — predators and prey mingle without fear. In the background, a river landscape with trees and distant figures is visible. Text inscriptions from Isaiah surround the image as borders.
theological_description: Edward Hicks's Peaceable Kingdom is one of the most beloved visual meditations on Isaiah's messianic prophecy: 'The wolf shall dwell with the lamb, and the leopard shall lie down with the young goat, and the calf and the lion and the fattened calf together; and a little child shall lead them' (ISA.11.6). This vision of cosmic reconciliation looks both backward and forward — backward to Eden, where Adam lived in harmony with creation, and forward to the eschatological restoration when Christ's reign will heal all enmity. The child at center is the messianic figure described by Isaiah: 'a little child shall lead them.' The Peaceable Kingdom envisions what Paul describes as the creation waiting 'with eager longing for the revealing of the sons of God' (ROM.8.19), when 'the creation itself will be set free from its bondage to corruption.' This is not merely an animal fable but a vision of the complete reconciliation accomplished by Christ — the new Adam who restores the dominion over creation that the first Adam lost.

scripture_refs.reason: The wolf dwelling with the lamb and the child leading them — the direct source of the image
The wolf and lamb feeding together, the lion eating straw — the eschatological restoration
Creation groaning and waiting for liberation — the cosmic peace depicted here
The original peaceable dominion of Adam over creation, restored in the messianic age
